title: Rebecca Sokol-Snyder
text:
Hi, I’m Rebecca.


Data scientist with 5+ years of experience managing end-to-end analysis projects and turning messy, unstructured data into scalable data systems. I work across Python, R, and SQL to build reproducible pipelines, automate reporting, and communicate findings for diverse audiences. Currently pursuing a Master of Data Science at the University of British Columbia with a focus on applied machine learning and statistical analysis. I’m drawn to mission-driven organizations making an impact on people’s lives, not just the bottom line.

View my projects → About me

title: How We Reduced Reporting Time by 60% with a Tableau Automation
text:
Every Monday morning, one analyst on our team spent roughly three hours pulling numbers from several source systems into a spreadsheet, formatting it, and emailing it to a dozen stakeholders. It was error-prone, nobody was confident the numbers matched, and the data was already a week old by the time it landed in inboxes.
I was asked to look into automating it.

title: How We Reduced Reporting Time by 60% with a Tableau Automation
text:
Every Monday morning, one analyst on our team spent roughly three hours pulling numbers from several source systems into a spreadsheet, formatting it, and emailing it to a dozen stakeholders. It was error-prone, nobody was confident the numbers matched, and the data was already a week old by the time it landed in inboxes.
I was asked to look into automating it.

title: How We Reduced Reporting Time by 60% with a Tableau Automation
text:
Scoping it out
The first step was understanding what the report actually needed to answer — which turned out to be different from what was being calculated. A few stakeholder conversations revealed that most readers only cared about three metrics; the other eighteen columns were rarely looked at.
I mapped the data lineage in SQL to find the authoritative source tables, then sketched a simpler schema that could drive a live view.






Note



I kept a rough data dictionary in a shared doc throughout — this was genuinely useful when handing off to the team later.

title: How We Reduced Reporting Time by 60% with a Tableau Automation
text:
The build
The solution ended up being a fairly standard two-layer architecture: a set of SQL views that cleaned and joined the source data, feeding a Tableau workbook connected via a published data source.
-- Simplified example of the aggregation logic (table/column names changed)
SELECT
    week_start,
    region,
    COUNT(DISTINCT entity_id)   AS active_count,
    SUM(metric_value)           AS total_value,
    AVG(metric_value)           AS avg_value
FROM cleaned.weekly_activity
WHERE status = 'active'
GROUP BY 1, 2
The Tableau side was deliberately kept simple — three KPI tiles at the top, one trend line, one breakdown table. I resisted the urge to add more.

What the dashboard looked like
The screenshot below is blurred to avoid showing company data, but the general layout is visible:



The finished dashboard — three KPI tiles and a regional breakdown table.


Screenshot blurred. Hover to see layout.

title: How We Reduced Reporting Time by 60% with a Tableau Automation
text:
Results
After a two-week parallel-run to verify numbers matched, the Monday morning process was retired. The rough time saving was around 60% — mostly from eliminating the manual pull and the back-and-forth when numbers didn’t match.
A secondary benefit I didn’t anticipate: stakeholders started checking the dashboard mid-week rather than waiting for the Monday email, which led to a couple of early catches on data issues.

title: How We Reduced Reporting Time by 60% with a Tableau Automation
text:
What I’d do differently
Start with the data model, not the viz. I built a first version of the Tableau workbook before I’d fully settled the SQL layer, which meant reworking calculated fields twice. The time I spent on that would have been better spent upfront in SQL.
Document the refresh schedule explicitly. We had a confusing week where stakeholders thought the data was live when it was actually refreshing once daily. Clearer labelling on the dashboard would have avoided this.

Tools used: SQL Server · Tableau Desktop · Tableau Server
Context: analytics role at a regional retail organisation
